evidence: sync-check.sh conflates two distinct cases under the same 'Policy drift detected' message: (1) CLAUDE.md does not exist (absent-file path, triggered on fresh clone since CLAUDE.md is gitignored), and (2) CLAUDE.md exists but content mismatches. Case 1 is not drift — there is no prior file state to drift from. The condition [ ! -f "$CLAUDE_MD" ] fires the misleading message. Also reveals a test-gap: no end-to-end test for first-Stop-after-install behavior.
proposed_handoff: dispatch rabbit-cage feature owner to: (1) add E2E test asserting absent CLAUDE.md does not emit 'drift detected', and (2) split sync-check.sh systemMessage into distinct 'first run' vs 'drift' branches
